title: Groq AI Tool Cheat Sheet
description: A practical cheat sheet for engineers using the Groq AI tool, focusing on API parameters, CLI flags, and prompt techniques.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content where diverse outputs are desired.

commands.description: Selects the Groq-3 model with streaming output.
commands.scenario: When you need real-time responses in a chat application.

commands.command: "Explain the concept of quantum computing in simple terms."
commands.description: Prompts the AI to simplify complex topics.
commands.scenario: When creating educational content for a general audience.

commands.description: Limits the response length to control output verbosity.
commands.scenario: When integrating with platforms that have character limits.

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourages logical reasoning and verification in responses.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for debugging purposes.
Defines sequences where the AI should stop generating further text.
commands.scenario: When you need to ensure responses end at logical points.

commands.command: "Generate a list of potential risks for this project."
commands.description: Prompts the AI to identify risks in project planning.
commands.scenario: When conducting a risk assessment for a new project.

commands.description: Reduces the likelihood of repeating the same line verbatim.
commands.scenario: When generating varied and non-repetitive content.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent long waits.
commands.scenario: When ensuring responsiveness in a time-sensitive application.

commands.description: Prompts the AI to provide a concise summary of a given text.
commands.scenario: When needing quick overviews of lengthy documents.

commands.description: Encourages the model to introduce new topics in the conversation.
commands.scenario: When you want the AI to explore diverse ideas in brainstorming sessions.

commands.description: Prompts the AI to translate text into French.
commands.scenario: When creating multilingual content for international audiences.

commands.description: Retries the API request up to 3 times on failure.
commands.scenario: When ensuring reliability in network communication.

commands.description: Nucleus sampling parameter to control the cumulative probability of the next token selection, useful for balancing diversity and coherence.

commands.command: system_prompt="You are a senior DevOps engineer. Output only JSON."
commands.description: Defining a system role to enforce strict output formats and persona-driven responses.
Requesting the log probabilities of the tokens to evaluate model confidence and uncertainty in specific outputs.
Implementing a sliding window strategy to manage long-form conversations and prevent context overflow on LPU inference.
Providing 3-5 input-output pairs within the prompt to guide the model toward a specific complex pattern or syntax.
Limits the sampling pool to the top K most likely next tokens to reduce tail-end noise and improve coherence in technical outputs.
Enforces strict JSON output structure at the decoding level, preventing the model from including conversational filler outside the JSON block.
Prompting technique: 'Generate an initial response, then identify potential inaccuracies, then verify each point against facts to provide a final corrected version.'
Leveraging Groq's speed to execute multiple function calls in a single turn by defining multiple tool definitions in the tools array.
Analyzing 'usage' object fields (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens) to calculate cost-per-request and optimize context window utilization.
